source.filename: Linear IgA Bullous Dermatosis (Mild Localised) AMS-026.txt

attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 AMS (Autoimmune Mild Severity) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all AMS casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: Provisional case_id SPEC-DERM-03-00226 — SEQ derived from source number AMS-026; maintainer/ingest --reseq to confirm/assign the real sequence within the SPEC-DERM-03 bucket.
Specialty inferred as DERM (Dermatology) from the dermatological presentation; reviewer may prefer an immunology/allergy tag.
Difficulty inferred as red_herring_laden: the alarming SJS/anaphylaxis mimics are red herrings that must be actively excluded from a mild localised eruption.
Age (42) inferred with NO source signal — the note gives no age; conservative adult value assigned pending clinician confirmation.
Sex_at_birth (female) inferred with NO source signal — the note gives no sex; schema requires the field, so a value was assigned and must be confirmed on review.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source is an in-person home assessment (dermatological exam, Nikolsky sign, mucosal survey, clinician-measured vitals). These clinician-only findings were sealed into 10/11 with telehealth_limits; the presentation layer was de-anchored to a frightened patient describing blisters before any diagnosis is known.

Baseline tier set to T3 (urgent same-day in-person assessment) rather than the source's post-examination 'GP within 24-48h', because telehealth cannot perform the exam that excludes SJS/TEN; reviewer to confirm the reprojected tier.
No dose/frequency given in the source for the potent topical corticosteroid; dose_route_frequency omitted rather than invented (a specific agent/strength is left for the in-person prescriber).

Candidate SNOMED/ICD-10-AM codes (LABD 402313007 / L13.8, SJS 73442001, anaphylaxis 39579001, bullous impetigo 399183005) are unverified — pending terminology receipt; the LABD SNOMED concept in particular should be confirmed against the AU edition.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
